steps.title: Identify the sentence core
steps.content: The sentence needs to name the two gaseous compounds and then connect those names directly to the plural verb "have." The word "compounds" gives the category, while "quindoxin and isobutylene" gives the two names that match the two molar masses listed with "respectively."

steps.title: Check each punctuation pattern
steps.content:
• Choice A - compounds, quindoxin and isobutylene,: the second comma separates the two compound names from the verb "have," making the names read like a parenthetical interruption.
  • Choice B - compounds quindoxin and isobutylene,: the comma before "have" incorrectly separates the plural subject from its verb.
  • Choice C - compounds, quindoxin and isobutylene: the comma separates the category label from the two compound names, and no comma separates those names from the verb "have."
  • Choice D - compounds quindoxin and isobutylene: without a comma after "compounds," the category label runs directly into the first name, making the structure unclear.
The answer is C.
